setting.note: Richard Lamb loose in the Banda Oriental, the purple land east of the river: he lands at Montevideo, rides north through Canelones and Florida toward an estancia on the Paysandú border, and is swept into Santa Coloma's doomed revolt across the Cuchilla Grande and the coast of Rocha. Hudson rode this ground himself, and it brings Uruguay, and South America, into the atlas.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. W. H. Hudson, first published 1885; the author died in 1922.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1922).
rights.attribution: W. H. Hudson, The Purple Land (1885). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: A purple land may be found in almost
palette.note: The purple of Hudson's own title for the Banda Oriental, taken straight from his preface. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

santacoloma.name: General Santa Coloma
santacoloma.colorSourceQuote: greener and the flowers brighter for that crimson shower
